questions.prompt: What is your primary citizenship (passport)?

options.text: European Union (Schengen)
USA / Canada / UK / Australia
Other (requires visa processing for most countries)

questions.prompt: What is your preferred legal status for your second home?

options.text: Tourist status is enough (stays up to 90 days)
Digital Nomad / Independent Worker Visa
Permanent residency via investment (Golden Visa / property purchase)
Pathway to full citizenship

questions.prompt: How long do you plan to stay in this location at a time?

options.text: A few weeks a year (short vacations)
3 to 6 months a year (overwintering / seasonal living)
More than 6 months a year (it will become my primary tax residency)

questions.prompt: In the event of a global crisis (e.g., closed airspace), how do you need to access this location from your current home?

options.text: Exclusively by land (car, train within the same continent)
By air (distance is not an issue)
By sea / boat

questions.prompt: What is your tolerance regarding the host country's political regime?

options.text: I only accept fully developed Western democracies
I don't mind hybrid regimes or authoritarian states if they guarantee internal order and safety for expats (e.g., Singapore, UAE)
I don't care about the regime, only my personal freedom on my property

questions.prompt: How important is the country's military and strategic isolation (the "Nuclear Escape" factor)?

options.text: Critical – I want a country outside NATO/major military blocs, ideally in the Southern Hemisphere or an island nation (e.g., New Zealand, Fiji, Chile)
Moderate – I prefer neutral European countries or stable regions (e.g., Switzerland, Austria)
Not at all – I want to be in the center of global events (USA, Western Europe)

questions.prompt: What is your stance on government regulations and restrictions (e.g., lockdowns, mandatory vaccination during pandemics)?

options.text: I require a country with a history of mild or no sweeping restrictions (e.g., Sweden, certain US states)
I respect strict government measures if they protect society
It doesn't matter to me

questions.prompt: How sensitive are you to the risk of asset expropriation or currency reform?

options.text: Extremely sensitive – I require a strong rule of law and 100% protection of private property
I am willing to take risks in a developing country in exchange for lower costs and high returns

questions.prompt: Does your profession/business allow for the legal transfer of income abroad?

options.text: Yes, I work fully online / live off investments
No, I am tied to the physical location of my current home

questions.prompt: What level of petty street crime (theft, assault) is acceptable to you?

options.text: Near zero – I want to be able to leave my car/house unlocked (e.g., Japan, Iceland)
Low – standard awareness is enough (most of Europe)
Average – I don't mind areas with "bad neighborhoods" as long as I know where not to go

questions.prompt: How concerned are you about organized crime and corruption?

options.text: I require a country with a transparent government and minimal corruption
Bureaucratic corruption doesn't bother me if it allows me to "speed things up"

questions.prompt: What is your stance on the right to self-defense and gun ownership?

options.text: I want to live in a country where it is legal and straightforward to own a firearm for self-defense (e.g., USA, Czechia)
I prefer a country with strict gun bans among the civilian population (e.g., UK, Japan)
I have no strong opinion

questions.prompt: How important are cybersecurity and internet freedom in the given country?

options.text: Critical – I refuse any web censorship, VPN blocking, or user surveillance
I don't mind moderate regulation as long as it doesn't block standard work tools

questions.prompt: How do you view social unrest and the risk of demonstrations/strikes?

options.text: I am looking for a country with an extremely calm, homogeneous, and non-confrontational culture
I don't mind a dynamic society where protests are a normal part of politics (e.g., France)

questions.prompt: How much weight do you give to the safety of women and children (if traveling with family)?

options.text: Key factor – family safety is my absolute number one priority
Standard requirements – typical family-friendly environments

questions.prompt: What is your tolerance for a heavy military or police presence on the streets?

options.text: I prefer a country where police are not visible unless needed
Increased presence of armed forces makes me feel safe (e.g., Israel, Singapore)

questions.prompt: What temperature spectrum suits you best for most of the year?

options.text: Constant heat (above 25 °C year-round, tropics)
Mediterranean ideal (warm summers, very mild winters around 10–15 °C)
Four distinct seasons (including snow in winter and hot summers)
Year-round cool / mountain climate (temperatures rarely exceeding 20 °C)

questions.prompt: How do you handle high humidity?

options.text: I love it / don't mind it (typical of Southeast Asia or the Caribbean)
I prefer dry heat (Mediterranean, desert regions like Arizona or Dubai)

questions.prompt: What is the minimum number of sunny days per year you require?

options.text: Maximum – I want permanent sunshine (over 300 days a year)
I don't mind alternating clouds, rain, and sun (temperate zone)
I like overcast skies, fog, and a Nordic atmosphere

questions.prompt: What is your relationship with precipitation and the rainy season?

options.text: Monsoons or regular heavy rains don't bother me as long as the landscape is green
I want a country where it rains minimally and predictably

questions.prompt: How concerned are you about the impacts of climate change (drought, extreme heatwaves)?

options.text: Extremely – I am looking for a "climate refuge" (areas that will remain stable long-term, e.g., Northern Europe, southern tip of South America)
Moderately – I expect it, but I will manage it with AC / heating

questions.prompt: What is your tolerance for the risk of hurricanes, typhoons, and cyclones?

options.text: Zero – I want regions where these phenomena do not structurally occur
I accept seasonal risk if the local infrastructure is of high quality

questions.prompt: How do you perceive the risk of earthquakes and volcanic activity?

options.text: I require tectonically completely quiet areas
I don't mind living in a seismically active zone (e.g., Japan, Iceland, Costa Rica)

questions.prompt: Is the length of daylight in winter important to you?

options.text: Yes, I suffer from a lack of light – I need long days in winter (closer to the equator)
No, I don't mind short winter days (Nordic countries)

questions.prompt: What must be in the immediate vicinity of your home?

options.text: Ocean / sea coast
High mountains / ski resorts
Deep forests / lakes / pristine nature
No nature – I want a concrete jungle and skyscrapers

questions.prompt: At what altitude should the location be situated?

options.text: At sea level (coastal plains)
Mid-altitude (foothills, rolling hills)
High mountain areas (above 1500 m above sea level)

questions.prompt: What type of beaches do you prefer (if you want the sea)?

options.text: Tropical sandy beaches with warm water for swimming
Wild, rocky coastlines with a cold ocean (for views, surfing)
I don't insist on beaches, a view of the water is enough

questions.prompt: How important is proximity to water sources (rivers, fresh drinking water)?

options.text: Critical – I want to live in an area with a massive natural supply of fresh water
Standard – I rely on the municipal water supply

questions.prompt: Are you looking for an island or the mainland?

options.text: Island – I want a clearly defined, isolated territory
Mainland – I want the option to get in a car and drive thousands of miles in any direction

questions.prompt: What view from the window is most important to you?

options.text: A view of the water surface
A view of mountain peaks
A view of the city skyline and lights
A view of greenery / garden

questions.prompt: How much do insects and wildlife (snakes, spiders) bother you?

options.text: They bother me – I want areas with minimal dangerous or annoying fauna
They don't bother me – I see it as a natural part of an exotic environment

questions.prompt: What size of human settlement suits you best?

options.text: Megacity (millions of inhabitants, 24/7 life)
Medium-sized city (50–500k inhabitants, full amenities, less hustle)
Small town / village (community life)
Absolute solitude / off-grid (nearest neighbor out of sight)

questions.prompt: What architectural style of the surroundings do you prefer?

options.text: Ultra-modern (skyscrapers, glass, steel, futurism)
Historical / European (ancient centers, stone streets)
Traditional local / exotic (colonial style, Asian architecture, wooden structures)
Looks don't matter, functionality comes first

questions.prompt: What mode of transport do you primarily plan to use locally?

options.text: Exclusively my own car (I require great roads and parking)
Walking + top-tier public transport (subway, trams)
Scooter / motorcycle (typical for Asia/Mediterranean islands)

questions.prompt: How far are you willing to commute for basic amenities (groceries, pharmacy)?

options.text: Within a 5-minute walk
Within 15 minutes by car
I don't mind driving an hour once a week for a big shop

questions.prompt: How important is walking distance to cafes, restaurants, and culture from your home?

options.text: Essential – I want to live an urban lifestyle
Secondary – I'd rather walk in the woods; I'll drive to a restaurant

questions.prompt: How do you perceive noise pollution?

options.text: I hate it – I require absolute day and night silence
City sounds don't bother me; on the contrary, they energize me

questions.prompt: What should the population density be in the given region?

options.text: Very low (I want space for myself, cheap land)
High (I want to be where business and trends are made)

questions.prompt: What standard of internet connection is critical for you?

options.text: Top-tier – fiber internet with low latency and 5G coverage (for trading, video calls)
Stable average – reliable DSL or fast 4G is enough
Independent – as long as satellite Starlink works over the location, I'm good

questions.prompt: What is your requirement for international airport proximity?

options.text: Within 45 minutes drive (I travel often, need a hub with direct flights globally)
Within 2 to 3 hours drive (I accept longer transfers in exchange for more peace)
I don't need an airport / a local domestic runway is enough

questions.prompt: What level of healthcare do you require locally?

options.text: Absolute top-tier – internationally accredited hospitals, English-speaking doctors
Standard – regional hospitals on a good European/American level
Basic – first aid; for serious cases, I have insurance with repatriation (flight home)

questions.prompt: How reliable must the power grid and energy supply be?

options.text: 100% stable without outages (Western standard)
I don't mind occasional short blackouts if the house has a backup generator
I plan to have my own solar/wind system, I don't care about the grid

questions.prompt: What is your requirement for tap water quality?

options.text: I want tap water to be drinkable and high-quality without filtration
I don't mind buying bottled water or using reverse osmosis

questions.prompt: How important is the presence of Amazon / fast delivery of online purchases?

options.text: Very – I shop online constantly, want delivery within 2 days
Secondary – I can manage without it, local shops are enough

questions.prompt: Do you use an electric vehicle (EV) or plan to buy one?

options.text: Yes, I need a developed infrastructure of charging stations
No, I prefer an internal combustion engine / petrol / diesel

questions.prompt: What language do you need to communicate in locally?

options.text: English only (I require English to be the main or widely spoken language)
I want Spanish / Portuguese (or am willing to learn them quickly)
I am willing to learn any local language (e.g., Thai, Vietnamese, Polish)
Language doesn't matter, I'll use my hands and feet / translation apps

questions.prompt: What community of people do you primarily want to surround yourself with?

options.text: A community of international expats, digital nomads, and entrepreneurs
Local citizens (I want an authentic, local life)
I want to be entirely alone, I do not seek out people

questions.prompt: What is your relationship to religion in the host country?

options.text: I prefer a secular / atheistic environment
I want a traditional Christian cultural environment (Europe, Latin America)
I don't mind a Buddhist / Hindu environment (Asia)
I don't mind a Muslim environment (Middle East, North Africa)

questions.prompt: How important is local gastronomy and food culture to you?

options.text: Extremely – I want fresh ingredients, fish markets, fine dining, or great street food
Average – standard supermarkets are enough, I cook standard meals myself

questions.prompt: How do you view bureaucracy and the pace of life (the "Mañana" factor)?

options.text: I hate delays – I require efficiency, digitization, and punctuality (e.g., Germany, Estonia)
I like a slow living pace and don't mind things taking longer (e.g., Caribbean, Southern Europe)

questions.prompt: What is your stance on LGBTQ+ rights and the overall liberalism of society?

options.text: I require a highly liberal, open, and tolerant society
I prefer conservative, traditional family values
It doesn't matter to me as long as nobody bothers me

questions.prompt: Do you plan to live in the location with your pets (dog, cat)?

options.text: Yes, I choose the country based on how easy it is to import animals and how pet-friendly it is
No, I don't have pets

questions.prompt: How important is the availability of nightlife, clubs, and bars?

options.text: Very, I want to have fun late into the night
Not at all, I prefer sitting on the terrace or sleeping in the evening

questions.prompt: What is your maximum monthly budget to run the second home (rent/mortgage + utilities + food)?

options.text: Low-budget: Up to 1,500 USD per month
Mid-range: 1,500 – 3,500 USD per month
Premium: 3,500 – 7,000 USD per month
Luxury / Unlimited: Over 7,000 USD per month

questions.prompt: What is your primary goal from a taxation perspective?

options.text: Tax optimization – I want a country with zero income tax or territorial taxation (tax haven)
Fair tax – I don't mind paying taxes in exchange for great public services and infrastructure
I don't care about taxes, I won't be a tax resident in the country anyway

questions.prompt: Do you require the country to be cheap in terms of real estate purchases?

options.text: Yes, I want to buy a large plot/house for little money
No, the quality of the location dictates the price, I am ready to pay a premium

questions.prompt: In which currency do you want your local fixed costs to be pegged?

options.text: Only in strong global currencies (EUR, USD, CHF)
I don't mind local exotic currencies (THB, MXN, IDR) as long as inflation is low

questions.prompt: Are you looking for a property with potential for short-term rental (Airbnb) when you are not there?

options.text: Yes, the house must generate income when I'm away (requires local legislative support for Airbnb)
No, the house will serve exclusively me, privacy is absolute

questions.prompt: To what extent do you require food and energy self-sufficiency (the Prepping factor)?

options.text: High – I want a large plot with its own well, the possibility of growing crops, raising animals, and solar power
Zero – I want the full comfort of a city apartment without having to maintain anything

questions.prompt: What is your stance on the price and availability of local labor (nannies, cleaners, gardeners)?

options.text: I want a country where labor is cheap and I can easily afford full-time staff (e.g., Bali, Philippines, Mexico)
I don't use these services / I don't mind high Western rates for hourly work
